title: used to: past habits and states
lesson: 'used to + base verb' describes past habits or states that are no longer true. The negative is 'didn't use to' and the question is 'did … use to …?'

items.text: When I was a child, I {1} cartoons every morning.
items.explanation: Past habit: used to + base verb.

items.text: She {1} in a small village before moving here.
items.explanation: Past state: used to + base verb.

items.text: We {1} a lot of letters before email existed.
items.explanation: Past habit: used to + base verb.

items.text: He {1} smoke, but he gave it up last year.
items.explanation: 'used to' + the base verb 'smoke' already in the sentence.

items.text: I {1} like coffee, but now I drink it daily.
items.explanation: Negative: didn't use to + base verb 'like'.

items.text: {1} you {2} long hair when you were younger?
items.explanation: Question: did + subject + use to + base verb.

items.text: They {1} to school together every day.
items.explanation: Past habit: used to + base verb.

items.text: My grandfather {1} the piano beautifully.
items.explanation: Past habit: used to + base verb.

items.text: We {1} have a car; we always took the bus.
items.explanation: Negative: didn't use to + base verb 'have'.

items.text: She {1} much taller than her brother.
items.explanation: Past state: used to + base verb 'be'.

items.text: {1} they {2} near the beach?
items.explanation: Question: did + subject + use to + base verb.

items.text: I {1} afraid of the dark as a kid.
items.explanation: Past state: used to + base verb 'be'.

items.text: He {1} eat vegetables, but now he loves them.
items.explanation: Negative: didn't use to + base verb 'eat'.

items.text: We {1} every summer at the lake.
items.explanation: Past habit: used to + base verb.

items.text: My mum {1} bread every Sunday.
items.explanation: Past habit: used to + base verb.

items.text: {1} you {2} the guitar?
items.explanation: Question: did + subject + use to + base verb.

items.text: This building {1} a cinema many years ago.
items.explanation: Past state: used to + base verb 'be'.

items.text: I {1} so much TV; now I read instead.
items.explanation: Past habit: used to + base verb.

items.text: They {1} get on well, but they argue now.
items.explanation: Negative: didn't use to + base verb 'get'.

items.text: She {1} her bike to work every day.
items.explanation: Past habit: used to + base verb.

items.text: We {1} dinner together as a family.
items.explanation: Past habit: used to + base verb.

items.text: {1} he {2} in that office?
items.explanation: Question: did + subject + use to + base verb.

items.text: I {1} my grandparents every weekend.
items.explanation: Past habit: used to + base verb.

items.text: The shop {1} open on Sundays, but not anymore.
items.explanation: Past state: used to + base verb 'be'.

items.text: He {1} like spicy food when he was young.
items.explanation: Negative: didn't use to + base verb 'like'.

items.text: We {1} in a tiny flat near the station.
items.explanation: Past state: used to + base verb.

items.text: She {1} the violin in the school orchestra.
items.explanation: Past habit: used to + base verb.

items.text: {1} you {2} to that café often?
items.explanation: Question: did + subject + use to + base verb.

items.text: I {1} taller than my dad, but he caught up.
items.explanation: Past state: used to + base verb 'be'.

items.text: They {1} games outside until it got dark.
items.explanation: Past habit: used to + base verb.

items.text: He {1} drink milk; now he's lactose-free.
items.explanation: 'used to' + the base verb 'drink' already in the sentence.

items.text: We {1} travel by train, but now we fly.
items.explanation: 'used to' + the base verb 'travel' already in the sentence.

items.text: {1} she {2} glasses at school?
items.explanation: Question: did + subject + use to + base verb.

items.text: I {1} call my friends; now I just text them.
items.explanation: Past habit: used to + base verb.
